skill.name: youtube-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube search result metrics and channel details through the BrowserAct YouTube workflow API.
skill.summary: Collect structured YouTube video and channel metrics from keyword searches.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Manual review confirms the skill performs intentional network requests to BrowserAct and reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY for authorization. The critical heuristic is not confirmed as malicious: no prompt injection, hardcoded secret, subprocess execution inside the Python script, weak cryptography, or unrelated data exfiltration was found. Publish with a warning that the skill runs a local script and sends user queries plus a BrowserAct bearer token to BrowserAct.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Third-Party API Requests
medium_findings.description: The bundled Python script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as a bearer token to the BrowserAct API. This is required for the skill, but it exposes the user's BrowserAct credential and search terms to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly retrieves BROWSERACT_API_KEY and uses it in Authorization headers for requests to api.browseract.com. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is credential exposure to the intended provider rather than confirmed malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Executed Local Python Script
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run scripts/youtube_api.py with user-provided keywords and an upload date. The script does not spawn subprocesses, but marketplace users should know that use requires local script execution and network access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill documentation explicitly directs execution of the bundled Python file, and the Python entry point consumes command-line arguments. No shell command construction appears in the script, which lowers the severity.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded BrowserAct Service URLs
low_findings.description: The script and documentation contain fixed BrowserAct URLs. These URLs are expected for the BrowserAct workflow service and API key setup, but they confirm the skill depends on an external network service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible and are used for the documented BrowserAct integration. They are not suspicious by themselves, but they are strong evidence of third-party network dependency.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives in Documentation and Identifiers
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged Markdown backticks, API key wording, weak crypto terms, and task ID logging. These lines document expected parameters or print a BrowserAct task identifier; I did not find evidence of dynamic code execution, weak cryptography, prompt injection, or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found the flagged SKILL.md backticks are Markdown formatting and examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. The weak-crypto and reconnaissance hits are not supported by the reviewed lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Third-Party API Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The bundled Python script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as a bearer token to the BrowserAct API. This is required for the skill, but it exposes the user's BrowserAct credential and search terms to a third-party service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly retrieves BROWSERACT_API_KEY and uses it in Authorization headers for requests to api.browseract.com. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is credential exposure to the intended provider rather than confirmed malicious exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Executed Local Python Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to run scripts/youtube_api.py with user-provided keywords and an upload date. The script does not spawn subprocesses, but marketplace users should know that use requires local script execution and network access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill documentation explicitly directs execution of the bundled Python file, and the Python entry point consumes command-line arguments. No shell command construction appears in the script, which lowers the severity.

content.value_statement: YouTube research often requires slow manual collection of video and channel metrics. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow to return structured metrics from keyword searches.
actual_capabilities: Runs a bundled Python script for BrowserAct YouTube workflow tasks.
Searches YouTube by keyword with upload date filters.
Polls BrowserAct task status until completion or timeout.
Returns video titles, URLs, publish dates, views, duration, comments, and likes.
Returns channel titles, channel URLs, and subscriber counts when available.
Reports missing or invalid BrowserAct API key conditions to the user.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY before use.
Sends search terms and the BrowserAct bearer token to BrowserAct.
Supports only the upload date filters documented in the skill.
Depends on BrowserAct task availability and YouTube page data returned by that workflow.

use_cases.title: Track Competitor Video Performance
use_cases.description: Collect recent video metrics for competitor topics and compare views, likes, comments, and publishing patterns.

use_cases.title: Find High-Engagement Tutorials
use_cases.description: Search current YouTube tutorials for a topic and identify videos with strong engagement signals.

use_cases.title: Build Research Datasets
use_cases.description: Gather structured YouTube video and channel fields for market research, reporting, or enrichment workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Search Recent Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to find YouTube videos for "AI agent tutorials" uploaded this week. Return titles, URLs, views, likes, comments, and channel names.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Topic Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube for "browser automation" videos from this month. Summarize the strongest videos by engagement and note channel subscriber counts.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Campaign Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect YouTube metrics for "product launch demo" from this week. Group the results by channel and highlight videos with unusually high comments or likes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Research Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube for "enterprise AI workflow" from this month. Produce a concise research brief with top videos, engagement patterns, and data quality caveats.

output_examples.input: Find recent YouTube videos for "AI agent tutorials" uploaded this week.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of matching videos with channel names, video URLs, publish dates, durations, views, likes, comments, and subscriber counts where available.

output_examples.input: Monitor competitor videos about "browser automation" from this month.
output_examples.output: A comparison summary showing which channels published relevant videos, how each video performed, and which results need follow-up review.

output_examples.input: Collect campaign video metrics for a product launch keyword.
output_examples.output: A clean table-style summary of video performance fields and a short note about missing or delayed metrics.

best_practices: Use precise keywords to reduce unrelated YouTube results.
Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY only in the local environment or approved secret store.
Review returned YouTube metrics before using them in public reports.
anti_patterns: Do not provide unrelated secrets when the skill asks for a BrowserAct API key.
Do not use broad keywords when a focused campaign or topic is required.
Do not assume returned metrics are complete when BrowserAct or YouTube reports missing fields.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to run the BrowserAct YouTube workflow.

faq.question: Does it call YouTube directly?
faq.answer: No direct YouTube API call is visible. The script sends workflow requests to BrowserAct.

faq.question: What data can it return?
faq.answer: It can return video, channel, publishing, duration, view, like, and comment metrics when the workflow provides them.

faq.question: Can it filter by upload date?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented filters are Today, This week, This month, and This year.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is not blocked, but it should publish with a medium-risk warning for script execution, network access, and API key use.

faq.question: What should users avoid sharing?
faq.answer: Users should avoid sharing unrelated credentials, private keywords, or sensitive campaign terms unless BrowserAct use is approved.
